title: Maximum Subarray
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, find the contiguous subarray (containing at
least one number) with the largest sum and return that sum.

solutions.summary: Either extend the running subarray or start fresh at each element.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Seed with the first element
steps.explanation: Both the best sum and the current running sum start at `nums[0]`.

code.python:
def max_sub_array(nums):
    best = nums[0]
    current = nums[0]
    return best

steps.title: Loop from the second element
steps.explanation: Process the remaining values one at a time.

steps.title: Extend or restart
steps.explanation: The best subarray ending here is either this number alone or this number added to the previous run.

steps.title: Track the overall best
steps.explanation: Keep the largest running sum we have seen anywhere.

code.python:
def max_sub_array(nums):
    best = nums[0]
    current = nums[0]
    for n in nums[1:]:
        current = max(n, current + n)
        best = max(best, current)
    return best

solutions.summary: Sum every subarray and keep the largest total.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Seed the best with the first element
steps.explanation: Use `nums[0]` so all-negative arrays are handled.

steps.title: Choose a start index
steps.explanation: Each subarray begins at some index `i`; reset a running total there.

steps.title: Extend to each end index
steps.explanation: Grow the subarray to index `j`, adding each value to the running total.

steps.title: Track the largest total
steps.explanation: Each running total is a real subarray sum; keep the maximum.
